highlights.text: The most common of these building blocks is what I call elevator down sell-into-short squeeze.
ES cycles between two types of price action: 1) Elevator down sells (this is where ES sells in a non-stop straight line).
These occur after elevator down sells and see ES put in a face-ripping squeeze.
These are caused by my core setup, the Failed Breakdown.

After going elevator down all day Thursday I warned readers on Thursday at 4pm that “The squeeze on this selloff is still pending - but it will come when get the trigger.” On Friday 9:30AM we got that trigger.
Yesterday we spent the session selling afterwards going elevator down into the afternoon down to new lows at 6658.
Readers know this means we need to be waiting for the Failed Breakdown squeeze trigger.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “The Failed Breakdown of today’s 6670 low is obviously of interest.” We lost 6670 yesterday afternoon down to 6658, recovered it, longs triggered, and we squeezed into the close.
Overnight, we lost this and went elevator down.
This was a deep elevator down sell, so readers know what comes next.
The Failed Breakdown and short squeeze.

I wrote yesterday: “Some supports to watch would be 6605-10 and 6593.” We hit 6593 by 11am to set the low of day, recovered 6605-10 (Failed Breakdown), and put in the short squeeze to 6690ish before dipping into the close.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the setup that caused Friday’s face ripping 100 point squeeze (it was a Failed Breakdown) as well as multiple smaller Failed Breakdowns we had yesterday (incredibly, we had 5 just from Friday’s close to late day yesterday).
I wrote yesterday: “ If we lose today’s lows and don’t recover very quickly, we begin a leg down that should see 6605-10 minimum but take it level to level.” By 10am this morning, we hit 6605-10.

post_title: 4 Red Days In A Row For SPX, Second Time Since April. Can Bulls Go Green Tomorrow? Nov 19 Plan
